comment.body:
@Dmnk thank you for your kind words, only smart and hard work pays out for sure. i'm glad you like the content, i try to be as educative as i can be. 
Skew..
i will cover skew in depth later in educational posts, but the key is that smirk is caused of the increased leverage and left-tail hedging at the OTM put wings. OTM contracts do have higher IV than ATM, but skewed to left-tail hedges. 
Smirk examines the IV along the moneyness, with the same maturity. 
We have different smirks for each maturities, and we want to know the slope to see the supply/demand ratio changes over the time (how the bets move).

comment.body:
but to answer your question @Ryan , liquidity premium can compress when market conditions are stable (see Fama–French three-factor model).
in a controlled vol environment this liquidity premium might not widens that much, allowing small caps to perform better relative to larger caps. 
Also, empirically deregulation favors for them, as it reduces costs for smaller firms.
Also note, that the beta of small caps can be higher, telling that if vol environment is stable, they can provide alpha in the short-run. 

Tech led positive momentum can provide spillover effect on small caps. 
this is why rotation occurs, and long dispersion, short correlation, short vol.

comment.body:
@Alec Pradhan your Q about delta = % prob of option end up ITM.
It is not true, you can check it on ToS too, that sometimes they are close but not equals.

it is bcs delta is N(d1)
and the risk–neutral probability of expiring ITM is N(d2), where d2 = d1 - sigma * sqrt(Time)

Now, if sigma or time is low.. or when we are near to ATM, d2 and d1 can be very close to each other.
but never equal

I have a related question... what vol shall we track and in what timeframe, to observe the vol/spot beta??

Alma uses VIX's IV and RV a lot in her posts, and the timeframe is daily. But I assume the intraday movement also matters when the price is about to spin at or breakthrough the zero Vanna point, isn't it?
